design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Peter Pan in the Hook film universe, navigating platforming challenges

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Cheat code allows skipping levels entirely via map selection, suggesting developer accommodation for difficulty frustration

progression.unlock_mechanism: Map-based destination selection; cheat code enables free map access bypassing level completion
replayability_hooks: Hidden ending sequence accessible via cheat code
Multiple regional versions with different credits and high score entry lengths

world_building_techniques: Licensed film IP (Peter Pan Hook film from 1991)
Map-based world navigation suggests themed environments

sources_used: tcrf - Hook (NES) - The Cutting Room Floor
extraction_notes: Source material focused on hidden development text, regional differences, and cheat codes rather than gameplay mechanics. Insufficient documentation to determine difficulty parameters, boss design philosophy, or core design patterns. Cheat code implementation (Right, Up, Up, Down, Down, Right, Left, Up + Select) indicates developer consideration for player accessibility and replayability.
